metadata.summary: pytest with pytest-django is the modern Django test stack. Tests use fixtures (not setUp), factory_boy for test data, APIClient.force_authenticate for auth, and the db fixture for database access.

content.title: Django Testing with pytest
content.summary: pytest with pytest-django is the modern Django test stack. Tests use fixtures (not TestCase), factory_boy for test data, APIClient.force_authenticate for auth, and the db fixture for database access. Every test is a function or class method — no TestCase inheritance. Block all real network calls with an autouse socket monkeypatch.

why.p: pytest fixtures compose and override cleanly where TestCase.setUp does not. Parametrize replaces repeated test methods. factory_boy generates realistic data with field overrides in a single call. @pytest.mark.django_db makes DB access explicit, preventing accidental hits. The result is faster, more readable tests that survive refactoring because they test observable behavior, not internal method calls.

list.item: New Django projects — pytest-django is the default over TestCase
Adding cross-cutting fixtures (API client, authenticated user, factory) reused across many tests
Integration tests against DRF endpoints with APIClient and JWT/session auth
Testing services, models, and database operations
Adding negative tests (auth failures, validation errors)
Projects already heavily invested in TestCase where mixed styles cause confusion — pick one
Test environments without DB access (pure logic) — plain pytest without pytest-django is lighter
Async test suites using httpx.AsyncClient against ASGI directly — db fixture and sync ORM complicate things; use pytest-asyncio with manual setup
Code paths relying on call_command-style isolation where TestCase transaction handling is simpler

rule.statement: Set DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE in pyproject.toml [tool.pytest.ini_options] to a test-specific settings file with DEBUG=False, in-memory cache, fake email backend, and MD5PasswordHasher.
rule.rationale: Test settings reduce overhead and prevent accidental real email/payment sends. MD5PasswordHasher speeds up tests by 10-100x but must never leak into dev/prod settings.

example.title: pytest Configuration
example.code: [tool.pytest.ini_options] DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE = "config.settings.test" python_files = ["test_*.py"] python_functions = ["test_*"] addopts = ["-v", "--tb=short", "--strict-markers", "-ra"] markers = [ "slow: deselect with '-m not slow'", "integration: integration tests", ]

rule.statement: Keep conftest.py close to where fixtures are used. Project-root conftest only for truly global fixtures (api_client, user, auth_client, network-blocking).
rule.rationale: Auto-discovery cascades — nested conftest fixtures shadow each other silently if not scoped.

section.name: shared-fixtures

rule.statement: Add an autouse network-blocking fixture in the root conftest that raises on any socket.create_connection call. Tests that need real network must explicitly opt out.
rule.rationale: Tests that forget to mock external services produce 30s timeouts, not clear failures. A loud guard makes the gap obvious immediately.

section.reference: Minimal conftest with api_client, user, auth_client, and network-blocking fixture.

section.name: factory-pattern

rule.statement: Use factory_boy DjangoModelFactory instead of fixture-as-function for test data. Override fields per test with UserFactory(email='x@y.z').
rule.rationale: factory_boy handles sequences and related objects; plain fixtures-as-functions require manual wiring for each variant.

example.title: Factory Pattern
example.code:
import factory from apps.users.models import User
class UserFactory(factory.django.DjangoModelFactory): class Meta: model = User email = factory.Sequence(lambda n: f"user{n}@example.com") name = factory.Faker("name")

section.reference: DjangoModelFactory scaffolds for User and related models.

rule.statement: Mark test classes or functions with @pytest.mark.django_db only when DB access is required. Pure logic tests must not touch the DB.
rule.rationale: DB setup costs time; pure logic tests run much faster without it. --strict-markers makes missing markers a hard error.

rule.statement: Structure each test: Arrange (set up data) → Act (call code under test) → Assert (verify observable outcome). One logical assertion group per test.
rule.rationale: Tests with multiple unrelated assertions fail for multiple reasons simultaneously, making the root cause ambiguous.

example.title: Unit Test Structure

rule.statement: Use APIClient.force_authenticate instead of issuing real login requests in integration tests — much faster and tests the endpoint, not the auth flow.
rule.rationale: Real login calls include password hashing and token generation — 100-1000ms per test. force_authenticate bypasses all of that.

example.title: API Integration Test

rule.statement: Use @pytest.mark.parametrize for boundary and equivalence-class testing where the same logic runs against multiple inputs.
rule.rationale: Replaces repetitive test functions with a single declaration; failures show exactly which parameter set failed.

example.title: Parametrized Test

rule.statement: Mock at the I/O boundary (HTTP, mail, payment), test the rest with real DB and real serialization. Over-mocking produces tests that prove nothing.
rule.rationale: Mocking internal methods tests implementation, not behavior. Tests that mock the wrong import path pass silently with zero real coverage.

rule.statement: Use transactional_db (not db) when the code under test calls transaction.on_commit — on_commit callbacks never fire inside a rolled-back transaction.
rule.rationale: db wraps each test in a savepoint that never commits; on_commit handlers that send emails or dispatch tasks are silently skipped.

antipattern.description: Testing internal method calls instead of observable behavior.
antipattern.reason: Tests that assert on _validate_items was called break on any refactoring that renames the method, even if behavior is unchanged. Test the outcome (exception raised, object created) not the path.

antipattern.description: Shared mutable state on test class attributes (class-level list or dict).
antipattern.reason: Tests execute in arbitrary order; shared state causes order-dependent failures. Use pytest fixtures — each test gets a fresh instance.

antipattern.description: Re-creating fixtures inline instead of reusing shared conftest fixtures.
antipattern.reason: Inline creation duplicates logic and diverges from the shared fixture over time. LLMs must read conftest.py first and reuse user, api_client, auth_client.
Masks migration drift — agents update models without re-running migrations, and existing DB state hides the missing column. CI must always --create-db.

antipattern.description: Calling mocker.patch with the module the agent read, not the module where the SUT imports the symbol.
antipattern.reason: Patching the wrong import path produces a test that always passes regardless of the mock — silent zero coverage.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: A test-writing subagent reads the spec/test-plan, lists test cases per acceptance criterion, then generates pytest functions using shared fixtures from conftest.py. The agent NEVER assumes a fixture exists — it greps conftest.py first. A test-runner subagent runs pytest --junitxml=results.xml -q --cov=src --cov-fail-under=80, parses results, and feeds failures back. A code-review agent enforces "no @pytest.mark.django_db without db fixture if you only read", "no transactional_db if db is enough", and "no setUp style — use fixtures."

Test generation prompt pattern: Generate pytest tests for apps/app/services/file.py. Use existing fixtures from tests/conftest.py (read it first). For each public function: 1 happy-path + 1 boundary + 1 error case. Use APIClient and the user fixture. Mark integration tests @pytest.mark.integration. Output the full file. Stop.

Test result parsing: Run pytest --junitxml=/tmp/r.xml --cov=src --cov-report=term-missing -q. Parse failures. For each: test name, root cause (code/test/fixture/flake), proposed fix as a unified diff. Do not apply yet.

section.name: limitations-and-failure-modes
section.p: db fixture wraps each test in a transaction (fast) but breaks if the code-under-test calls transaction.on_commit — those callbacks never fire. Use transactional_db and pay the speed cost.
pytest-django's live_server fixture spawns a real server; flaky on CI without explicit wait_for_port and parallel-port allocation.
pytest-xdist parallel runs trip on shared DB state; need --create-db per-worker plus DB name templating. pytest-xdist + pytest-django + --create-db + --reuse-db is finicky.
Auto-discovery of conftest.py cascades — duplicate fixtures in nested conftests silently shadow each other.
factory_boy + DRF + recursive relations (User → Profile → User) cause infinite recursion if SubFactory cycles are not broken with LazyAttribute.
pytest-mock mocker.patch("module.attr") patches the wrong scope when the code under test imports the symbol differently than the test does — silent test pass with no real coverage.

rule.statement: Require agents to read tests/conftest.py before generating any test. Prompt: "Read tests/conftest.py first. Reuse existing fixtures."
rule.rationale: LLMs default to inline creation rather than reuse; reading the file first surfaces the available fixture names.

rule.statement: Require --strict-markers and a lint rule that finds DB-touching code in tests without @pytest.mark.django_db.
rule.rationale: LLMs omit the marker; the resulting error is clear but wastes tokens iterating. Catch it in lint, not at runtime.

section.name: best-practices
section.p: Always supply on_delete= explicitly on ForeignKey — PROTECT for "do not lose data", CASCADE only for owned children, SET_NULL for optional refs.
Add related_name= on every FK; if a reverse accessor is not needed, use related_name='+'.
Put Meta.ordering only on small / paginated models; on large tables it adds an ORDER BY that hurts performance.
Combine Meta.indexes with a real EXPLAIN against representative data — do not add indexes speculatively.
Use models.UniqueConstraint(fields=[...], name='...') over unique_together (deprecated path).
Use TextChoices for fixed enums; reference them through a constants module so admin/forms/serializers all share one source.
Never edit applied migration files; create a new migration to alter or drop instead.

template.purpose: Minimal conftest with api_client, user, auth_client, and network-blocking fixture.
factory_boy DjangoModelFactory scaffolds for User and related models.
